nameMeanings.name: Gower
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name for someone from Gohiere, the Old French form of what is now known as Goelle, the country north of Paris.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Anglo-Norman French Goier, Joier, denoting a man from any of several places in Normandy and Picardy called Gouy, Joué, or Jouy (derived from the Latin personal name Gaudius, Old French Goi, Joi + -acum ‘joy’).
English (of Norman origin): from a Norman personal name, Go(h)ier, ancient Germanic Godehar, cognate with the Old English name mentioned at Gooder .

variants.name: Gooder
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from a Middle English personal name Godere, Old English Gōdhere, composed of the elements gōd ‘good’ + here ‘army’.
English: alternatively, a variant of Goodair, a nickname from Middle English god(e), gu(o)d ‘good’ + heir(e), (h)air(e) ‘heir’.

variants.name: Gowers
meanings: English (Essex and Suffolk): variant of Gower , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Gurr
meanings: German: derisive nickname for a useless person or a wicked woman, from Middle High German gurre ‘bad mare’.
English (Kent and Middlesex): variant of   Gower   or   Gore  .

variants.name: Jowers
meanings: English (Essex and Suffolk): variant of Gower , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Goward
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Gower , with excrescent -d.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Gower was born about 1420, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Gower, Esq, was 32 and his mother, Joan, was 22. He married Katherine Sutton about 1425. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1459, in Droitwich, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Gower est né environ 1420, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Gower, Esq, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Joan, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Katherine Sutton environ 1425. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1459, à Droitwich, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Gower um 1420 in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Gower, Esq 32 und seine Mutter Joan 22. Er hat um 1425 Katherine Sutton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1459 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Droitwich, Worcestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Gower è nato il circa 1420 nel Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Thomas Gower, Esq, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Joan, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Katherine Sutton circa 1425. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1459, nel Droitwich, Worcestershire, England, all'età di 40 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Gower(이)가 대략 1420년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Gower, Esq(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1425년 Katherine Sutton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1459년 영국 워스스터실르 드로이트위치에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.
